name: Kavitha Nair
tagline: If Kama Ayurveda changes their rose water formula, I will personally write to the CEO

demographics.education: M.A. English Literature from Kerala University
demographics.occupation: Senior Teacher at a CBSE school in Ernakulam

aspiration_drivers: giving her daughters the best she can
brands that feel like an extension of her identity
trust_signals: brands she has used for 5+ years
recommendations from her sister and close friends
rejection_triggers: new brands trying too hard to be trendy
aggressive social media marketing that feels pushy
emotional_hooks: brands that do not need to shout

backstory: Kavitha grew up in Thrissur surrounded by her grandmother's Ayurvedic remedies — coconut oil infused with hibiscus, turmeric face packs, and neem water. When she discovered Kama Ayurveda in her late twenties, it felt like those traditions packaged beautifully. She has used their rose water for eight years straight and their kumkumadi oil for six. She does not chase trends. She finds what works and sticks with it. Her daughters are now old enough to start their own skincare routines and she is guiding them toward the same brands — not because she cannot try new things, but because she genuinely believes consistency is how you build good skin.
current_life_context: Her eldest daughter asked for a 'Korean skincare routine' after watching YouTube videos. Kavitha is skeptical but willing to learn. She is also noticing that Forest Essentials recently raised their prices significantly and is mildly irritated — loyalty should be rewarded, not taxed. She might consider one new brand if it has a strong Ayurvedic story.
inner_monologue_style: Calm, considered, occasionally traditional. Thinks: 'I have been using this for eight years and it has never let me down. Why would I switch because some Instagram girl says so? But let me see what this new brand is about — if they have real Ayurvedic roots, maybe I will try one product. Just one. And if it disappoints, I am going straight back.' Mixes English with Malayalam expressions when emotional.
